MY 2020 HYUNDAI TUCSON WAS PURCHASED NEW THIS YEAR FROM WEST HERR HYUNDAI I'M WILLIAMSVILLE NY. I WAS NOTIFIED OF A VEHICLE SAFETY RECALL RELATED TO ABS ISSUES IN SEPTEMBER 2020. I HAVE CONTACTED THE DEALER EVERY WEEK SENSE THEN, AND THEY CLAIM THEY ARE UNABLE TO COMPETE THE RECA

TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 HYUNDAI TUCSON. THE CONTACT RECEIVED NOTIFICATION OF NHTSA CAMPAIGN NUMBER: 20V543000 (SERVICE BRAKES, HYDRAULIC) HOWEVER, THE PART TO DO THE RECALL REPAIR WAS NOT AVAILABLE. THE CONTACT STATED THAT THE MANUFACTURER EXCEEDED A REASONABLE AMOUNT OF TIME

RECEIVED RECALL 195 NOTICE PERTAINING TO ELECTRICAL SHORT IN ABS SYSTEM AND THAT VEHICLE SHOULD NOT BE PARKED IN GARAGE OR NEAR ANY STRUCTURES DO TO POTENTIAL FIRE.  WENT TO DEALER FOR INSPECTION, WAS TOLD THAT PART WOULD BE IN AND REPLACED IN A WEEK TO TEN DAYS.  THREE WEEKS LAT

narrative.headline: 2020 Hyundai Tucson: engine and brake complaints lead the list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 167 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 39%, followed by the service brakes (26%), electrical system (11%), hydraulic system (10%), and forward collision avoidance (7%). No crashes, injuries, or deaths are reported, and only one complaint involves a fire (under engine). The top areas to check on a test drive are the engine and brakes.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 65 reports (39%), including 1 with a fire. No crashes or injuries reported. Worth a careful look on a test drive.

issues.blurb: Second most reported: 44 reports (26%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Test the brakes thoroughly.
